content.claim: While free public transport for children up to age 14 was introduced in March 2023, rural and small-settlement transport frequencies remain limited, particularly in North-Eastern, South-Western, and Western Hungary—regions that overlap with areas of highest demographic depopulation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Transport frequency data are qualitative (e.g., 'limited') rather than quantified (e.g., buses per day)
Overlap between high-depopulation regions and limited-transport regions is inferred from combining two sources, not directly stated
National public transport ridership decline (35% bus passengers 2001–2023) is aggregate; rural-specific trends not provided
Acceptability of transport substitution for primary-school students by age and distance not assessed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
